recommendedApproach: Build the Sagemark SEO Creator as Approach B (native AI SDK v6 agent loop) in a new apps/seo service, ported from origin/preview rather than re-authored from scratch, and explicitly borrow the fine-tune/client-review UX from Approach A's session model. Concretely: (1) the deterministic moat — the 22 scorers, the cross-model faithfulness gate (sonnet drafter vs haiku verifier), the non-compensatory seo-gate (Stage-A vetoes then Stage-B 8-dim composite), the fail-closed lifecycle-fsm, and the content_clients/content_pieces/content_piece_versions/voice_specs Drizzle schema — is PULLED from origin/preview (PRs #1668-1684, verified present there and absent locally) and registered as host-side platform primitives, NOT reimplemented; (2) the four runtime SKILL.md producers (seo-strategist, seo-assistant, seo-blog-writer, seo-audit) are re-authored as AI SDK v6 system prompts + typed tools, with the live Whispering Willows hub captured as a golden set BEFORE any prompt is written; (3) generation runs as a ToolLoopAgent on a normal Vercel Node/Fluid route using the trailhead resolveGatewayModel seam + fail-closed CostAccountant (both verified in apps/trailhead/src/lib/ai.ts), with the scorers/gate exposed to the agent as READ-ONLY tools while Stage-A vetoes and canPublish() are enforced in host code the agent can never reason past; (4) the agent UI is a three-zone canvas (Agent | rendered-piece preview | Inspector/gate scorecard) that streams real token deltas, with post-generation fine-tune as conversational multi-turn edits, each accepted edit writing a content_piece_versions snapshot and re-running the full gate. Do NOT default to Approach A (Managed Agents / Claude Agent SDK) — the literal Agent SDK is non-serverless and Managed-Agents beta access is unconfirmed for the org. Do NOT default to Approach C (Vercel Sandbox) — the artifact is content (markdown + JSON-LD + a deterministic gate), not runnable code, so the one thing only a Sandbox can do is unused. Reserve A/C as a later flagged "true autonomous research agent" tier if literal SKILL.md execution ever becomes non-negotiable.
recommendationRationale: B wins on the one axis that decides this: it delivers the genuinely agent-driven, Claude-harness-feeling UI with real token streaming and native multi-turn fine-tune (the user's #1 and #2 requirements) on infra ALREADY PROVEN in-repo — ai@6, @ai-sdk/anthropic, @ai-sdk/gateway, the resolveGatewayModel provider seam, and the fail-closed CostAccountant are all live in apps/trailhead today (verified). A and C both chase literal-SKILL.md-execution fidelity that is either non-serverless (Agent SDK), beta-gated and unconfirmed (Managed Agents), or pays microVM cost+latency+ops for a code-execution capability this content product never uses (Sandbox). The decisive insight from the bibles is that the LLM is the replaceable middle and the moat is the harness — the gate, the grounding contract, the fail-closed FSM, the YMYL governance, the per-client tenancy — and that harness ports IDENTICALLY regardless of runtime, so the cheapest runtime that can host a real agent loop wins. B borrows A's single genuine advantage (best-in-class fine-tune as a stateful conversational session) by adopting the artifacts-pattern side-panel editor + conversational edits + append-only versions, and it borrows from both A and C the discipline that the deterministic core is engine-agnostic and must be built first. Critically, the research established that the content_pieces/seo-gate/lifecycle-fsm/render engine is NOT greenfield — it exists on origin/preview and must be ported, not reinvented, which collapses B's biggest stated cost (re-authoring) down to just the four LLM prompts. The two non-negotiable disciplines that keep B on-thesis: enforce Stage-A vetoes + canPublish() in host code outside the agent loop (the agent gets read-only scoring tools, never a path past a YMYL/faithfulness/thin-content veto), and capture Whispering Willows as a golden set before writing a single prompt so methodology-fidelity regression — the failure mode that won't show up in CI — is caught on every prompt/model bump.

topLevelDecisions.choice: Native AI SDK v6 ToolLoopAgent on a Vercel Node/Fluid route (Approach B), using the trailhead resolveGatewayModel seam + fail-closed CostAccountant. NOT Claude Agent SDK (non-serverless), NOT Managed Agents (beta-unconfirmed), NOT Vercel Sandbox (artifact isn't runnable code).
topLevelDecisions.rationale: ai@6 + @ai-sdk/anthropic + @ai-sdk/gateway and the provider seam are already proven in apps/trailhead/src/lib/ai.ts (verified). It runs in-process on Vercel, streams real token deltas, and gives Gateway cost/usage observability the hand-rolled OpenRouter fetch lacks. No new infra, no beta dependency, no microVM ops surface.
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: A1 dedicated Node sidecar (reintroduces process supervision, secrets, a second deploy target — the component most likely to fail silently); A2 Managed Agents (cleanest literal-harness path but beta access unconfirmed, materially higher per-run cost); C Vercel Sandbox (unproven/unprovisioned scaffold, microVM latency before first token, code-execution capability unused for content). Keep A2 as a flagged later tier if literal SKILL.md execution becomes mandatory.

topLevelDecisions.choice: PORT the deterministic moat (scorers, faithfulness gate, seo-gate, lifecycle-fsm, content_clients/pieces/versions/voice_specs schema) verbatim from origin/preview PRs #1668-1684; RE-AUTHOR only the four runtime producer prompts (strategist/assistant/writer/audit) as system prompts + typed tools.
topLevelDecisions.rationale: The engine is framework-agnostic pure functions and already exists on origin/preview (verified: local schema-flywheel stops at videogen 0029; content_pieces/seo-gate/lifecycle-fsm are not in the local tree). Porting collapses B's headline cost to just four prompts. The /seo-copywriter-build orchestrator is a build-time developer tool with no runtime role; its judge-prompt.md domain checks become the runtime acceptance-test spec.
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: Reimplement the engine from scratch (wasteful, re-introduces solved bugs); run the literal SKILL.md corpus in a host (A/C — the only approaches that avoid prompt re-authoring, but at the infra/cost penalties above).

topLevelDecisions.decision: The gate, and where it lives relative to the agent
topLevelDecisions.choice: Non-compensatory: Stage-A ordered hard vetoes (UNSOURCED_STAT, KEYWORD_STUFF, YMYL_NO_BYLINE, THIN_CONTENT, BANNED_LEXICON, VOICE_FAIL, EVAL_FAILED) evaluated FIRST and short-circuiting to REJECT/REVISE with score=null; Stage-B 8-dim 0-100 composite (faithfulness strictly heaviest, weights as config) only if Stage-A clean. Enforced in HOST code; the agent gets a read-only runGate tool only.
topLevelDecisions.rationale: The bibles' core thesis is gate-is-the-wedge and 'a perfect eval score makes a draft ELIGIBLE, not PUBLISHED.' If vetoes lived inside the LLM loop, a persuasive fine-tune instruction could talk past a YMYL/faithfulness veto. Hard gates are un-overridable canon; per-client thresholds/voice are instance overrides.
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: Soft advisory gates (today's shipped wizard reality — never blocks; wrong for a YMYL client); letting the agent self-grade (collapses the moat, the exact failure the bibles warn about).

topLevelDecisions.choice: Supabase Postgres as system of record, mirroring the origin/preview Drizzle schema; every row scoped by workspace_id + client_id with fail-closed RLS (anon SELECTs only status='published'). Resolve the YMYL byline server-side from content_pieces.author_id to the voice_specs author registry (close the known request.author trust hole). NOT localStorage.
topLevelDecisions.rationale: The shipped wizard's localStorage (cap 50) cannot do multi-tenant agency work; cross-tenant leakage is flagged the #1 agency-ending bug. Voice bleed (prose/facts traveling across clients) is the cardinal failure — namespaces must be a hard, fail-closed boundary.
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: localStorage-only v1 (fine for a single-operator demo, disqualifying for client work); trusting the agent to write the DB directly (keys must stay host-side, agent persists via a validated host-side persistPiece tool).

topLevelDecisions.choice: Conversational multi-turn edits in a three-zone agent canvas: artifacts-pattern side-panel markdown editor + chat; each accepted edit writes an append-only content_piece_versions snapshot, re-runs the full gate, and is guarded by SHA-256 stale-edit (409) + per-tenant rate limit + workspace-ownership checks (ported from videogen chat-edit). Client review = a tokenized hosted live preview of the actual SSR-rendered piece in a same-origin sandboxed iframe, reusing videogen's PinOverlay + PreviewClickHandler + useIframePinDrop for element-anchored pinned comments and ApprovalBeat for section-level Approve/Request-changes. Sign-off = a NAMED, undeletable version recording approver identity.
topLevelDecisions.rationale: This is the user's explicit ask: agent-driven, fine-tune the artifact, presentable to clients with on-screen feedback. Every reuse primitive (PinOverlay, PreviewClickHandler, useIframePinDrop, ChatEdit, VersionHub, ApprovalBeat) was verified present in the local tree, so this is copy-and-adapt, not net-new. A 'Request changes' comment routes straight into the edit loop as the agent instruction.
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: One-shot regenerate-from-scratch (today's wizard — fails the fine-tune requirement); PDF/raw-markdown handoff (client can't judge the page as Google/AI-crawlers will see it); a public Vercel-deploy toolbar-thread review (ties review to a per-client deploy and leaves the multi-tenant Supabase model).

topLevelDecisions.choice: Per-client SSR at /clients/[client]/blog/[slug] with the FULL body in initial HTML (mandatory — AI answer crawlers GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot largely don't run JS), FAQPage JSON-LD from faq_data, placeholder [photo:]/[cta:] resolution with unresolved-token stripping, per-client sitemap.xml/robots.txt, and a CI reachability gate (sitemap == published-and-indexable set, both directions). PLUS a net-new per-client resource-library HOMEPAGE template (hero, statistic callout, named three-stage cluster, guide-card grid, quality section, tour CTA + license badge) fed by the cluster map.
topLevelDecisions.rationale: The deliverable is a CLUSTER forming a hub, and the live Whispering Willows demo's homepage is a curated resource library the shipped engine does NOT render (it ships individual pieces + sitemap). CSR is lethal to the entire GEO thesis. Promote clusterRole/funnelStage to first-class columns to drive the homepage + related-guides nav (current schema persists neither edge).
topLevelDecisions.alternatives: Render individual pieces only (matches the engine but misses the demo's hub homepage the artifact ground-truth requires); keep cluster role in brief_snapshot only (insufficient for a true hub graph / related-guides nav).

fiveAnswers.interface: A three-zone agent canvas modeled on videogen's StudioCanvas, minus operator-only controls: LEFT = an Agent chat panel streaming real token deltas (live thinking + tool-use: 'fetching SERP, running faithfulness gate, Stage-A clean, scoring 8 dimensions') so it visibly behaves like the Claude harness; CENTER = the artifact, a side-panel markdown editor (artifacts pattern) for the content piece, toggling to a live SSR preview of the rendered page; RIGHT = an Inspector showing the two-stage gate scorecard (Stage-A vetoes as red blocking chips with stable codes, then the Stage-B 8-dim 0-100 bars with the verdict band, faithfulness visibly dominant) plus the version history. Generation is brief-first: the human reviews/refines the typed brief (intent observed from a live SERP fetch, cluster placement, entities, isYmyl/reviewerRequired flags), not the 2,200-word draft. Visuals match the existing apps/agents convention: Tailwind v4 foreground/background tokens, emerald/blue/amber/red verdict palette, font-mono eyebrow + font-display headings, inline Link pill tab-nav.
fiveAnswers.outputArtifact: The artifact UNIT is a content_piece — a long-form E-E-A-T markdown article carrying a cluster role (pillar | cornerstone | spoke/guide | FAQ | checklist) with meta_description, excerpt, faq_data (FAQPage JSON-LD), an 8-dimension scorecard, brief_snapshot, version, status, is_ymyl, and a credentialed author_id. The DELIVERABLE is a CLUSTER of ~8 such pieces forming a hub: one pillar ('Memory Care in Skagit County, WA') + funnel-staged spokes (Awareness to Consideration to Decision) realized as internal links + a curated resource-library homepage. Each piece carries intrinsic E-E-A-T (named accountable byline, source-grounding to named authorities, medical disclaimer, trust/license badge), GEO/AI-readiness (FAQ schema, question-style headings, answer-first capsules, quotable fact sentences), and SEO (topic clustering, pillar/cluster internal linking, local geo-targeting, funnel-stage keywords). It is content, not runnable code — which is precisely why Sandbox/Agent-SDK fidelity is unnecessary.
fiveAnswers.resemblesPriorWork: Yes — strongly, on two fronts, and explicitly NOT the retirement-pilot brochure site. (1) The ENGINE is the apps/agents content/seo-copywriter ContentEngine: the 22 deterministic scorers (flesch-kincaid, keyword-density, passive-voice, content-score, meta/og/faq generators), the cross-model faithfulness gate, the voice gate, and — on origin/preview — the seo-gate, lifecycle-fsm, and content_pieces/voice_specs schema. We port these, not reinvent. (2) The UI borrows the videogen agent canvas (PinOverlay, PreviewClickHandler, useIframePinDrop, ChatEdit, VersionHub, ApprovalBeat — all verified present locally) for streaming generation, conversational fine-tune, versioning, and client on-screen feedback. The output resembles the live Whispering Willows demo (a multi-page content hub) — a different deliverable from the removed retirement-pilot multi-page brochure site, which is single-tenant marketing copy with no gate, no cluster model, and no YMYL governance.
fiveAnswers.clientPresentation: As a tokenized, workspace-scoped hosted live preview of the actual SSR-rendered hub — the client judges the pillar page and guides exactly as Google, readers, and AI answer engines will see them (full body in initial HTML, FAQ JSON-LD, the curated resource-library homepage with hero/statistic/three-stage cluster/guide-card grid/tour CTA + license badge), paired with a SERP-snippet preview so they also see the search-result appearance. The client surface is deliberately separate from the operator surface: no credits, no Improve-Draft, no raw markdown export — a clean rendered page, a version history, and (for a premium client) a curated 'Reviewed & graded' trust strip. A PDF/print leave-behind (e.g. the printable 12-Question Tour Checklist) is a secondary export, not the review surface.
fiveAnswers.clientFeedback: On the hosted preview itself, at two granularities: (a) free-form pinned comments anchored to actual page elements (reusing PreviewClickHandler click-to-pin + PinOverlay markers + useIframePinDrop iframe anchoring, with a Vercel-toolbar-style thread model: pinned location + messages + open/resolved status); and (b) section-level Approve / Request-changes verbs on each H2 (reusing ApprovalBeat's pill + primary/secondary buttons). A 'Request changes' comment routes straight into the agent's conversational edit loop — the comment becomes the instruction, the agent emits a bounded body diff, applies it as a new auto-version, re-runs the full gate, and streams a one-line 'what changed' summary, so feedback becomes an auditable, reversible version rather than an email. Approval-cycle time and open-thread count ('approval debt') are instrumented per client, because the bibles name RELEASE — not generation — as the binding constraint. Client approval is advisory on hard gates: a client can never approve past a YMYL/faithfulness/thin-content veto, which stays non-overridable canon held by the credentialed reviewer.

phasing: Phase 0 (pre-work, ~0.5wk): Pull origin/preview into the working tree and capture the live Whispering Willows hub as the human-labeled GOLDEN SET before writing any prompt; lock the judge-prompt.md domain checks as the runtime acceptance-test spec.

Phase 1 — MVP / v0 'gate + one piece' (~3-4wk): Scaffold apps/seo + register a @sagemark/core (mirror trailhead's resolveGatewayModel + CostAccountant); port the scorer library + faithfulness gate + seo-gate + lifecycle-fsm + content_clients/pieces/versions/voice_specs schema from origin/preview as host-side primitives with workspace/client RLS; build a voice-spec editor with a hard-stop 'no approved spec means no generation' guard. Ship ONE grounded, gated, human-released piece with the agent loop (brief, draft, faithfulness, seo-gate) and the gate enforced in host code. Every publish is human; nothing auto-publishes.

Phase 2 — agent canvas + fine-tune (~2wk): Stand up the three-zone canvas with real token-delta streaming, the two-stage gate scorecard, conversational multi-turn fine-tune (append-only versions + SHA-256 stale-edit guard + per-tenant rate limit), and the side-panel markdown editor. Re-author + golden-regress the four SKILL.md producer prompts against the Whispering Willows set.

Phase 3 — render + hub + client review (~2-3wk): Per-client SSR /clients/[client]/blog/[slug] with full-body HTML + FAQPage JSON-LD + placeholder stripping + sitemap/robots + a CI reachability gate; the net-new resource-library homepage template fed by the cluster map (promote clusterRole/funnelStage to columns); the tokenized hosted client-review preview (pin stack + section Approve/Request-changes + threads + named-version sign-off + approval-debt KPI). Close the YMYL byline trust hole (resolve author server-side).

Phase 4 — v1 hardening / compounding loop (~2-3wk, partly deferrable): Multi-client tenancy proven end-to-end; cost ledger via Gateway usage measured against the <=$2 editorial target; observability (per-stage cost/latency, eval-score-distribution slide, cron heartbeats, kill-switch); then the compounding loop (freshness cron to draft, winner-amplification sibling briefs, GEO weekly query-bank) and DurableAgent checkpointing if the pipeline outgrows a single Fluid timeout.
biggestRisks: Methodology-fidelity regression: the re-authored prompts quietly produce lower-quality content than the SKILL.md harness, invisible to CI. Mitigation: capture Whispering Willows as a golden set BEFORE writing prompts and regress every prompt/model bump against it. This is the failure mode that decides whether B succeeds or rots.

Agent reasons past a hard gate: a persuasive fine-tune instruction bypasses a YMYL/faithfulness/thin-content veto if vetoes live inside the LLM loop. Mitigation: enforce all Stage-A vetoes and canPublish() in HOST code; the agent gets a read-only runGate tool only.

Cross-tenant leakage / voice bleed — the agency-ending bug: the agent's tools (serpFetch, retrieval, Supabase writes) or the tokenized review link widen retrieval across clients, or one client's prose/facts travel into another's piece. Mitigation: host-side custom tools keyed to one workspace_id/client_id, fail-closed RLS, review token scoped to exactly one piece/version, corpus namespace as a hard boundary.

Crawler-invisibility: any CSR slip in the rendered hub makes the body absent from the AI answer indexes the whole GEO thesis targets. Mitigation: SSR full-body as a CI-enforced reachability gate (sitemap == published-and-indexable set, both directions); apps/site currently has NO vitest and the 24 client-blog render tests were omitted, so placeholder-stripping / JSON-LD / status-filtering must be hardened before clients depend on it.

Under-scoping by reading only the local checkout: the content_pieces/seo-gate/lifecycle-fsm/render engine is on origin/preview (PRs #1668-1684), not local — the local tree only has the older single-piece ContentEngine. A plan that reads local code will badly under-estimate what already exists (good) and mis-locate the schema (bad). Mitigation: pull origin/preview first (Phase 0).

Cost/latency blowout: agent tool round-trips exceed the <=$2 editorial cap (a PRD target, not a measured number) and long pipelines exceed the Fluid window. Mitigation: measure from a live Gateway cost ledger; pre-flight cost reservation; adopt DurableAgent checkpointing before the pipeline outgrows one timeout.

YMYL credential hole inherited from origin/preview: the publish path trusts request.author rather than resolving content_pieces.author_id to the voice-spec registry — for memory-care content an uncredentialed byline could ship. Mitigation: resolve the byline server-side from persisted data at publish; require a named credentialed Person + citations + recorded human release before any YMYL piece publishes, with a backup-reviewer path.
openDecisionsForJames: Does the SEO Creator actually need an autonomous tool-using agent LOOP, or is a fixed brief-draft-gate sequence wrapped in nice streaming + conversational edits enough? The latter delivers ~90% of the value with far less nondeterminism risk and could save weeks — the agent abstraction is justified only if real multi-step tool use (SERP-ground, draft, score, revise in one loop) is wanted in v1.

Soft vs hard publish governance for the YMYL pilot: is the soft faithfulness badge acceptable for v1, or do we ship the full non-compensatory fail-closed gate + named-author/credentials/citations YMYL hard-stop now? (Recommendation: hard gate now — Whispering Willows is squarely YMYL and the gate IS the product — but this sets the v1 scope and timeline.)

Grounding source: stay on DuckDuckGo HTML scraping (3 pages, first 2000 chars — the product's weakest link) or fund a real SERP/retrieval API for the web app? Faithfulness is bounded by how good brief.sources are; this needs a budget/key decision.

Per-generation cost ceiling and the credits/wallet model: same videogen credits wallet or a separate SEO ledger, and what is the real blended per-client cost once the human editor + credentialed reviewer dominate? The whole pricing/margin story (15-20% of human cost) depends on measuring this from a live ledger.

Is Anthropic Managed Agents beta access worth pursuing as a flagged later 'literal-harness' tier, or do we commit fully to the native AI SDK path? (Recommendation: commit to native B; revisit Managed Agents only if literal SKILL.md execution becomes a hard requirement — and never the self-hosted Agent SDK / Sandbox for content.)

Reviewer capacity & backup-reviewer SLA: what pages/week can one credentialed reviewer actually clear, and who is the backup before any regulated client goes live? The bibles name the expert tier (not cost) as the binding constraint on YMYL publish rate — this is a business/staffing decision only James can make.

Does v1 own the per-client resource-library HOMEPAGE as a generated artifact (net-new template the engine doesn't render today), or hand-build it per client for the pilot and generate it later? This affects whether clusterRole/funnelStage get promoted to first-class columns in Phase 1 vs deferred.
